title: Keon Systems Runtime Verification Canon v1 Locked

product.primary_responsibility: Evaluate authority before consequential execution, produce verifiable authorization artifacts, enforce fail-closed governed execution, and preserve receipt-linked evidence for audit and offline verification.

purpose.summary: Repo-verification canon for evaluating whether the Keon Systems Runtime implementation supports the claims made about governed execution, Decision Receipts, PolicyHash, fail-closed enforcement, evidence production, and CAES-aligned conformance.
purpose.verification_default: A claim is not accepted unless supported by implementation evidence, contract/schema evidence, and meaningful tests or executable verification receipts.

in_scope: Decision Receipt creation and verification semantics
Tenant, actor, authority, and delegation binding
Runtime evidence envelopes and evidence packs
Runtime-managed governed event recording or Runtime integration contract for governed spine append
Offline verification artifacts exposed by Runtime
CAES Level 1, Level 2, and Level 3 claim gating as implemented by Runtime
Runtime APIs, services, contracts, schemas, tests, and CI checks that prove the above
out_of_scope: MCP Gateway transport implementation except where Runtime exposes or requires a contract consumed by the Gateway
Cortex storage internals except where Runtime emits receipt, spine, or causal evidence contracts consumed by Cortex
Collective cognition, deliberation, planning, branch collapse, heat, witnesses, or swarm behavior
Website copy, pitch decks, sales collateral, and UI page content unless they make Runtime conformance claims
General platform deployment features not involved in governed execution

integration_boundary_notes.runtime_relevance: Gateway may call Runtime for Decide and Execute, but this canon evaluates Runtime-side authority semantics, contracts, and guarantees only. Gateway-specific transport, MCP protocol handling, headers, structuredContent, and client compatibility belong in the MCP Gateway canon.
Runtime may emit receipt-linked records intended for Cortex or a governed spine. This canon verifies Runtime emission contracts and fail-closed requirements, not Cortex persistence internals.

Collective may submit governed execution candidates to Runtime through approved boundaries. This canon verifies Runtime cannot be bypassed and does not grant cognition execution authority. Collective planning and cognition are separate canon targets.

source_alignment.source: Keon Runtime Product Feature Canon v1.2

core_doctrine.statement: Effects are governed. Runtime must not permit any effect-bound execution path that bypasses authority evaluation and receipt verification.
Receipts outrank stories. Logs, summaries, UI narratives, and memory records are not authorization proof unless anchored to valid Decision Receipts and supporting evidence.
Missing anchors fail closed. If identity, authority, policy state, receipt validity, or execution lineage is missing or unverifiable, Runtime must deny or block execution.
Policy must be deterministic at the authorization boundary. LLM-only, prompt-only, or post-hoc governance cannot satisfy Runtime authorization semantics.

Runtime may integrate with Gateway, Cortex, or Collective, but Runtime remains responsible for its own authority-evaluation guarantees and must not rely on downstream narrative or storage layers to prove authorization happened.

artifact_hierarchy.rule: DecisionReceipt is the primary authorization artifact. EvidencePack, ExecutionPackage, Attestation, logs, narratives, and UI summaries derive from or reference receipts and must not override them.
artifact_hierarchy.verification_expectation: The repo must define or consume explicit contracts/schemas/types for these artifacts when claimed. Any public or code-level claim that an artifact exists must be backed by implementation and tests.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Decide before Execute
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must require an authorization decision before any effect-bound execution attempt.
acceptable_evidence: Service or pipeline code showing Execute cannot be reached before Decide approval
Contract or API shape separating decision from execution or explicitly enforcing decide_then_execute
Negative test proving denied decisions do not execute
Negative test proving missing decision state blocks execution
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Repo claims governed execution but exposes an execution path that does not require a prior approved decision.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Decision Receipt required before effect
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must produce or verify a valid Decision Receipt before an approved effect-bound action can execute.
acceptable_evidence: Receipt model/schema with required authorization fields
Execution guard requiring valid receipt reference or verified receipt object
Tests for missing receipt, malformed receipt, expired receipt, and receipt/action mismatch
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Execution can occur with only an approval boolean, log entry, or narrative explanation instead of a receipt-backed authorization artifact.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Cryptographic receipt signing
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must cryptographically sign Decision Receipts when claiming cryptographic proof or CAES Level 2 or above.
acceptable_evidence: Signing service, key material abstraction, or signer interface
Signature fields in receipt schema
Verification tests proving tampered receipt fails
Key rotation or key identifier handling when production-grade claims are made
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Repo claims cryptographic proof but receipts are unsigned, only hashed, or only logged.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: PolicyHash canonicalization
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must compute PolicyHash from canonical policy state using deterministic serialization/canonicalization before or during authorization evaluation.
acceptable_evidence: Canonicalization function or deterministic serializer
Hash algorithm explicitly defined
Tests proving equivalent policy state produces identical hash
Tests proving policy mutation changes hash
Receipt or evidence pack binds to PolicyHash
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: PolicyHash is random, timestamp-dependent, display-only, omitted from receipts, or computed from non-canonical input.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Receipt binds policy, identity, action, and time
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Decision Receipts must bind the evaluated policy state, tenant, actor or delegate, requested capability/effect, decision status, correlation identity, and evaluation timestamp.
acceptable_evidence: Receipt schema/type includes required binding fields
Decision service populates fields from inherited context
Tests for tenant mismatch, actor mismatch, action mismatch, policy mismatch, and replay mismatch
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Receipts exist but do not bind enough context to prove what was authorized.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Fail-closed policy evaluation
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Any failure in policy lookup, parsing, canonicalization, evaluation, dependency resolution, or policy service availability must deny or block execution.
acceptable_evidence: Exception/error paths return Denied, Blocked, Invalid, or equivalent non-executing status
Tests for policy unavailable, invalid policy, unknown capability, malformed constraints, timeout, and evaluator failure
No fallback to allow on policy errors
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Policy evaluation errors degrade to allow, best-effort execution, warning-only behavior, or post-hoc logging.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Fail-closed identity binding
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must deny or block when tenant, actor, authority, or delegation context is missing, inconsistent, unverifiable, expired, or outside scope.
acceptable_evidence: Identity binding validator
Tenant and actor fields required in decision request or inherited authority context
Delegation validation logic when delegation is supported
Tests for missing tenant, missing actor, tenant mismatch, invalid delegate, expired delegation, and widened scope attempts
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Runtime defaults to anonymous, default tenant, system actor, or widened authority in production paths.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Capability and scope enforcement
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must evaluate requested capability/effect against tenant, actor, policy, and entitlement constraints before approval.
acceptable_evidence: Capability registry or policy binding model
Scope matching logic
Tests for missing scope, wrong scope, insufficient entitlement, unknown capability, and overbroad request
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Runtime authorizes based only on authentication or tool existence without capability-specific policy checks.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: No invocation-layer bypass
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must not expose alternate public, internal, worker, queue, admin, debug, or compatibility paths that perform effect-bound execution without governed authorization.
acceptable_evidence: Route/controller/job scan showing effecting paths traverse the same authorization guard
Static or architectural tests for direct executor usage
Tests or review evidence covering admin/debug/queue/batch paths
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Any route, command handler, queue consumer, background worker, or admin endpoint can execute effects directly.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Governed Spine event emission contract
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must define and honor a causal event emission contract for governed events when claiming Governed Spine support.
acceptable_evidence: Event model/schema for directive, intent, request, decision, execution, outcome, or Runtime-specific equivalents
Append or outbox contract preserving correlation and receipt references
Tests proving events are causally ordered and linked
Clear documented ownership if actual persistence belongs to another repo
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Repo claims append-only spine but only emits mutable logs or unlinked telemetry.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Spine append failure semantics
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: When Runtime is configured to require spine or evidence append, append failure must fail closed before effect execution.
acceptable_evidence: Configuration distinguishing required vs best-effort evidence persistence if both modes exist
Tests proving required append failure blocks execution
Tests proving best-effort mode is explicit and not marketed as full conformance
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Runtime claims full spine-backed governance but proceeds with execution after required evidence append failure.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Outcome receipt or terminal outcome evidence
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must produce terminal evidence for the final outcome of an approved, denied, failed, or blocked governed operation.
acceptable_evidence: Outcome receipt or terminal event schema
Decision and execution result models include terminal status
Tests for approved/executed, denied/not-executed, failed, blocked, and runtime-down outcomes
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Runtime records only approvals but not denied, blocked, failed, or terminal outcomes.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Denied decision never executes
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: A denied authorization decision must never invoke the executor, external adapter, downstream tool, queue publication, or side-effecting code path.
acceptable_evidence: Unit/integration test using spy/fake executor proving zero calls on deny
Control flow preventing executor invocation after deny
Terminal denied outcome evidence
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Denied path still calls executor, queues work, mutates external state, or relies on executor to self-deny.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Runtime dependency unavailable fails closed
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Unavailable policy, receipt, signer, evidence, entitlement, or downstream runtime dependency must not result in unauthorized execution.
acceptable_evidence: Tests for dependency timeout/unavailable/exception
Explicit blocked or denied status
No executor invocation when preconditions cannot be verified
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Runtime executes in degraded mode when required authorization or evidence dependencies are unavailable.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Offline receipt verification
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must expose or generate artifacts sufficient to verify authorization offline when claiming CAES Level 2 or offline-verifiable proof.
acceptable_evidence: Verifier API, CLI, library, or documented verification procedure
Receipt includes signature, PolicyHash, canonicalization metadata, key identifier, and enough bound fields
Test vectors or golden fixtures for valid and tampered receipts
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Repo claims offline verification but verification requires mutable server state, hidden logs, or undocumented manual trust.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Evidence pack completeness
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Evidence packs must contain or reference the receipt, PolicyHash, policy/evaluator version, identity binding, requested effect, decision result, terminal outcome, and correlation lineage.
acceptable_evidence: Evidence pack schema/type
Builder service populating fields from authorization and outcome paths
Tests that evidence packs include required references
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Evidence pack exists but is just a log bundle, UI summary, or unbound metadata collection.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Canonical policy protocol
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must implement or consume a canonical policy protocol with versioned policy templates/manifests, deterministic constraints, and local ratification semantics when claiming canonical policy governance.
acceptable_evidence: Policy manifest/schema/type
Template hash or policy version binding
Machine-evaluable constraints
Tests for manifest signature/hash mismatch and unratified policy rejection
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Runtime claims canonical policy protocol but accepts ad hoc prompt text or mutable local policy without deterministic manifest binding.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Policy evaluator determinism
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: For identical canonical input, policy state, identity context, and requested effect, Runtime policy evaluation must produce deterministic authorization output and PolicyHash.
acceptable_evidence: Deterministic evaluator implementation
Tests for repeated identical evaluations
Tests proving nondeterministic LLM-only decisions are not used as authority
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Authorization depends solely on nondeterministic model output, prompt interpretation, clock randomness, or unpinned external state.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Effect boundary classification
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must classify or receive classification for effect-bound actions so advisory-only operations are distinguishable from governed execution requirements.
acceptable_evidence: Effect boundary enum/model/schema
Policy rules keyed by effect class or capability
Tests proving effect-bound actions require receipts and advisory-only actions cannot masquerade as execution
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Runtime treats all requests the same and cannot prove which operations cross an effect boundary.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Immutable receipt record after issuance
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Issued Decision Receipts must be immutable in content and identity. Corrections or revocations must create new linked records rather than mutate prior receipts.
acceptable_evidence: Immutable receipt model or append-only storage/outbox semantics
Tests preventing update-in-place of issued receipt content
Revocation/correction model creates linked event
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Receipt content can be silently changed after execution.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Correlation lineage preservation
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must preserve correlation identity across decision, receipt, execution attempt, terminal outcome, and emitted evidence.
acceptable_evidence: Correlation ID in request/receipt/result/evidence schemas
Tests proving correlation survives decide and execute
Query or retrieval path by correlation ID where claimed
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Decision, receipt, execution, and outcome cannot be linked except by timestamp or manual inspection.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Public conformance statement gating
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime must not claim CAES conformance level, cryptographic proof, append-only spine, or offline verification unless the repo contains the required implementation and tests for that level.
acceptable_evidence: Conformance statement with CAES version and level
Mapped evidence for every claimed level requirement
CI/test receipts proving conformance gates
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Docs, README, site copy, metadata, or comments claim conformance beyond the implemented evidence.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: CAES Level 1 gate
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: To claim CAES Level 1, Runtime must prove all effect-boundary actions require verified Decision Receipts and fail-closed enforcement.
acceptable_evidence: RUNTIME-001 through RUNTIME-008 pass
RUNTIME-013 and RUNTIME-014 pass
Conformance statement cites CAES version and governed effect categories
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Runtime claims CAES Level 1 while any effect-bound action can execute without verified receipt or fail-closed enforcement.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: CAES Level 2 gate
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: To claim CAES Level 2, Runtime must satisfy Level 1 plus cryptographic signing, PolicyHash canonicalization, governed spine/evidence chain, and offline verification.
acceptable_evidence: RUNTIME-003, RUNTIME-004, RUNTIME-010, RUNTIME-011, RUNTIME-015, and RUNTIME-016 pass
Golden verification fixtures
Tests for tamper detection and append/evidence failures
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Runtime claims CAES Level 2 without signing, deterministic PolicyHash, append-only evidence semantics, or offline verification.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: CAES Level 3 gate
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: To claim CAES Level 3, Runtime must satisfy Level 2 plus explicit human authority delegation model, complete causal invariants, full spine specification, and effect classification.
acceptable_evidence: Delegation model and tests
Complete causal invariant test suite
Full spine specification or Runtime contract to an authoritative spine implementation
Effect classification tests and conformance statement
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Runtime claims full constitutional conformance while delegation, causal invariants, spine specification, or effect classification remain partial or deferred.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Advisory-only federation boundary
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: When Runtime participates in federation or cross-system proof exchange, it must preserve local authority and must not accept remote execution authority without local Runtime policy evaluation.
acceptable_evidence: Federation/proof exchange contract distinguishes advisory proof from local authority
Tests proving remote proof cannot bypass local policy
Docs clarify proofs shared do not transfer execution rights
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Runtime accepts external proof, agent request, or federation message as execution authority without local evaluation.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Execution package cannot outrank receipt
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Execution packages, run bundles, job records, or task envelopes must reference and remain subordinate to Decision Receipts.
acceptable_evidence: Execution package schema includes receipt reference and policy/hash binding
Tests preventing execution package creation without receipt
Docs or type comments identify receipt supremacy
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Execution package or job record is treated as sufficient authorization without receipt verification.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Attestation derivation
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Attestations must be derived from receipts and evidence, not independently asserted as truth.
acceptable_evidence: Attestation builder requires receipt/evidence inputs
Attestation schema references receipt/evidence IDs
Tests proving attestation cannot be produced from unverified narrative only
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Attestation can be generated from unverified summary, user claim, or mutable log only.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: Secure configuration for production proof claims
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Production paths must not rely on checked-in secrets, default signing keys, disabled verification, or dev-only bypass flags for proof claims.
acceptable_evidence: Configuration separates development and production modes
Tests or startup guards reject insecure production config
No checked-in private keys or static production secrets
Docs identify dev-only settings as non-conformant for production
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Runtime claims production-grade cryptographic governance while using dev keys, disabled verification, or permissive defaults in production mode.

runtime_verification_requirements.title: CI conformance gate
runtime_verification_requirements.requirement: Runtime repo should expose repeatable CI or local commands that run conformance-relevant tests for governed execution, receipts, PolicyHash, fail-closed paths, and claim gates.
acceptable_evidence: CI workflow or documented command
Focused test suite names or categories
Passing receipt from local or CI run
runtime_verification_requirements.false_claim_trigger: Conformance can only be asserted manually with no repeatable verification command.

required_negative_tests.title: Missing tenant or actor fails closed
Tenant or actor mismatch fails closed
Missing or insufficient capability scope denies
Required spine/evidence append failure blocks execution
Alternate route/job/worker cannot bypass Runtime guard
Remote federation proof cannot bypass local authorization

claim_status_rules.implemented_with_evidence: Requirement has direct code evidence, contract/schema evidence when applicable, and meaningful tests or executable receipts.
claim_status_rules.implemented_but_untested: Code appears to exist, but required negative or positive tests are absent or assertions are weak.
claim_status_rules.documented_only: Claim appears in docs/comments/README but implementation evidence is absent.
claim_status_rules.partial: Some implementation exists, but required binding, failure behavior, or artifact completeness is missing.
claim_status_rules.contradicted: Code behavior conflicts with the canon requirement.
claim_status_rules.false_claim: Repo or public-facing docs claim a capability/conformance level that the repo cannot prove.
claim_status_rules.ambiguous: Evidence is insufficient or unclear. In strict mode, treat as failing.
claim_status_rules.out_of_scope: Requirement belongs to another Keon repo or product canon and should not be used to fail Runtime.

verification_instructions_for_agents.strict_mode: Treat ambiguous evidence, documented-only claims, and missing negative tests as failing for critical/high requirements.
repo_review_order: Search public claims first: README, docs, package metadata, OpenAPI, schemas, comments, examples
Search Runtime APIs and service boundaries
Search policy evaluator and receipt services
Search tests and inspect assertions, not just names
Search CI workflows and conformance commands
Build requirement matrix with evidence citations
Suggest remediation lanes only if requested
evidence_rules: Do not treat docs as implementation proof.
Do not treat a test name as proof without inspecting assertions.
Do not treat logging as receipt generation unless receipt schema and verification semantics exist.
Do not fail Runtime for MCP Gateway transport gaps, Cortex persistence internals, or Collective cognition gaps unless Runtime itself claims ownership.
Do flag Runtime docs that claim capabilities owned by another repo without a clear integration boundary.
If a feature is marked deferred, do not classify it as false unless public claims present it as complete.
required_outputs: Runtime-specific Remediation Lane Plan if remediation is requested
Verification Receipt with commands, files inspected, tests run, pass/fail status, assumptions, and unresolved ambiguity

recommended_remediation_lane_groups.scope_hint: Policy evaluation, decision service, execution guard, deny/failure paths
Receipt schema, signer/verifier, PolicyHash canonicalization, offline verification fixtures
Tenant/actor/delegation context, authority inheritance, scope narrowing and widening guards
Governed event emission, evidence packs, terminal outcome receipts, correlation lineage
Static route/job scan, conformance statements, CI gates, public claim alignment

final_verdict_policy.conformant: All critical and high requirements pass with evidence, required false-claim guards pass for claims made, and no contradicted or false_claim findings remain.
final_verdict_policy.conditionally_conformant: Critical requirements pass, high/medium gaps are documented as not publicly claimed or explicitly deferred, and no false public conformance claims remain.
final_verdict_policy.non_conformant: Any critical requirement is missing, contradicted, or falsely claimed; or any CAES/proof/spine/offline-verification claim exceeds implemented evidence.
